data.title: Strive's Bitcoin-Backed Stock Sees Significant Trading Volume
data.description: Strive's Bitcoin-backed preferred stock, SATA, experienced substantial trading activity, with approximately $43 million exchanged in a single day after achieving its $100 par value. According to NS3.AI, Jeff Walton highlighted that JPMorgan's comparable variable-rate preferred stock traded around $5.9 million on the same day, despite having 3.5 times more shares outstanding than SATA.

data.title: CFTC Warns Prediction Markets on Insider Trading Laws
data.description: CFTC enforcement director David Miller has issued a warning that insider trading laws are applicable to prediction markets. According to NS3.AI, this announcement comes as the sector recently surpassed $20 billion in monthly volume, as reported by TRM Labs. Miller emphasized that the agency will exercise prosecutorial discretion, particularly targeting cases involving tipped or misappropriated information.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. Analysis: Bitcoin is approaching the "buy zone" but has not yet shown typical bottoming signals; 2. Brother Machi closed all his Bitcoin long positions one hour ago, and currently holds 6,800 ETH in his Ethereum long positions; 3. Whale "0x049b" opened 20x leveraged long positions in BTC and ETH, with current positions valued at over $80 million; 4. CoinShares will list on Nasdaq through the SPAC merger, valued at $1.2 billion; 5. PeckShield: LML suffered a hack, causing the token price to plummet by 99.6%; 6. Whale pension-usdt.eth opened another 3x leveraged short position in Bitcoin, currently holding 500 BTC; 7. The US Department of Justice indicted four crypto market makers for allegedly manipulating the market through "wash trading," with 10 people facing up to 20 years in prison.

data.title: LML Token Plummets After $950,000 Hack
data.description: According to PeckShield, the LML token suffered a significant hack resulting in a loss of approximately $950,000. Following the attack, the value of the LML token plunged by 99.6%. The attacker converted 950,000 USDT into 450.6 ETH and subsequently deposited the funds into TornadoCash.

data.title: PeckShield: LML token price plummets 99.6% due to hacking attack.
data.description: According to PeckShield, LML suffered a loss of approximately $950,000 due to a hack, causing the LML token to plummet by 99.6%. The attackers exchanged 950,000 USDT for 450.6 ETH and deposited it into TornadoCash.

data.title: New York City Lifts TikTok Ban on Government Devices
data.description: New York City has decided to lift its ban on TikTok for government electronic devices, allowing departments to resume posting on the platform. According to NS3.AI, a memo from the Mayor's Office stated that the change aims to enhance public communication. The memo also outlined requirements for using TikTok, including the use of dedicated devices, the exclusion of sensitive data, and the appointment of designated account managers.

data.title: KOSDAQ Circuit Breaker Activated as KOSDAQ150 Index Futures Surge 6%
data.description: South Korea's stock exchange implemented a circuit breaker following a 6% rise in KOSDAQ150 index futures. According to NS3.AI, this significant increase prompted the automatic trading halt to stabilize the market and prevent excessive volatility. Circuit breakers are mechanisms used by exchanges to temporarily halt trading in response to substantial price movements, ensuring orderly market conditions. The activation of the circuit breaker reflects heightened investor activity and market dynamics in South Korea's financial markets.

data.title: CoinShares will list on Nasdaq through a SPAC merger, with a valuation of $1.2 billion.
data.description: European cryptocurrency asset management firm CoinShares announced that it will begin trading on Nasdaq this Wednesday under the ticker symbol CSHR, through a merger with Vine Hill Capital's special purpose acquisition company (SPAC). The deal closed Tuesday evening, valuing the company at approximately $1.2 billion, including $50 million in institutional investment. CoinShares, founded 12 years ago, manages approximately $6 billion in assets and primarily serves institutional and retail investors, offering structured investment products and funds, including the CoinShares Bitcoin ETF listed in the US. CEO and co-founder Jean-Marie Mognetti stated that the US market has limited AUM (assets under management), and this listing will help the company rapidly expand into the US market through equity financing. (CNBC)

data.title: Indian Central Bank's Intervention Sparks Rupee Turmoil Amid Speculation Crackdown
data.description: The Indian central bank's unexpected crackdown on rupee put options last Friday evening has triggered a wave of emergency closures in the banking sector, with arbitrage trades worth up to $30 billion facing intense pressure. According to Jin10, as trading resumed on Monday, the market was gripped by liquidity panic, with insiders estimating that banks closed positions worth $4 billion to $10 billion that day. This leaves a significant portion of positions to be addressed before the April 10 deadline. With the Indian forex market closed on Tuesday and Wednesday due to holidays, Monday's closure frenzy led to the rupee's exchange rate plummeting to a historic low of 94.80, marking the largest daily fluctuation since 2013. The central bank's intervention is one of the most aggressive actions against currency speculation in a decade. Despite signals from U.S. President Donald Trump about a potential withdrawal from Iran, analysts from institutions like Wells Fargo warn that the rupee's fall to 100 per dollar is almost inevitable, driven by high oil prices from the Iran conflict exacerbating India's trade deficit and inflation. Currently, banks are forced to buy dollars domestically to cover offshore short positions, causing the domestic-foreign forward spread to soar to its highest level since 2020.

data.title: EU Proposes Centralized Oversight of Major Crypto Institutions
data.description: The European Union is advocating for the consolidation of regulatory authority over large cryptocurrency institutions under the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA). According to Foresight News, Malta has openly opposed this initiative. If the proposal advances through the EU Council and Parliament this summer, Malta may be required to relinquish its direct regulatory control over leading crypto entities to ESMA.

data.title: Israeli Air Force Conducts Extensive Operations Against Iran
data.description:
On April 1, the Israeli Defense Forces announced that since the commencement of Operation 'Roaring Lion,' the Israeli Air Force has executed over 800 strike missions. According to BlockBeats, these operations involved the deployment of approximately 16,000 various types of munitions, targeting over 4,000 sites and resulting in the deaths of more than 2,000 Iranian officers and soldiers.

The statement further detailed that alongside the strikes on Iran, the Israeli military has been conducting multi-front operations, having targeted around 7,000 sites across all its operational fronts to date.

data.title: The Korea Exchange activated its circuit breaker mechanism for the KOSDAQ index after KOSDAQ 150 futures rose 6%.
data.description: The Korea Exchange activated its circuit breaker mechanism for the KOSDAQ index after KOSDAQ 150 index futures rose 6%, suspending algorithmic trading for 5 minutes. (Jinshi)

data.title: New York City announces lifting of ban on the use of TikTok on government devices.
data.description: New York City Mayor Zuhran Mamdani announced on March 31 that the city would allow the resumption of use of the short-video social media platform TikTok on government electronic devices. On the same day, Mamdani posted a message on the platform saying, "TikTok, we're back." According to the Associated Press, a memo from the mayor's office shows that the New York City government is allowing departments to post content on the social media platform, a policy adjustment aimed at expanding the reach of the city government's information dissemination. The memo states, "We hope to open new channels for communication with the public and help deliver the information that New Yorkers need." The memo also requires that official departmental accounts adhere to a series of security measures, including using dedicated devices without sensitive data and designating specific personnel to manage the accounts. (Xinhua)

data.title: State-level crypto investment on the agenda in the US: Arizona House Rules Committee passes two crypto bills.
data.description: The Arizona House Rules Committee has passed two crypto-related bills, which are about to quickly move to a full House vote. SB1042 allows the state government to invest up to 10% of its funds in crypto assets; SB1649 proposes establishing a state-level digital asset reserve fund to provide institutional support for crypto investment and management. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: China Extradites Key Member of Criminal Group from Cambodia
data.description: China's Ministry of Public Security has announced the extradition of Li Xiong, a significant figure within the Chen Zhi criminal organization, from Phnom Penh, Cambodia. According to NS3.AI, Li Xiong previously held the position of chairman at Huione Group, a subsidiary of Prince Group. He is under investigation for alleged involvement in casino operations, fraud, illegal business activities, and concealing criminal proceeds.

data.title: Call for Clearer U.S. Rules to Boost Tokenization in Small Enterprises
data.description: Plume Network General Counsel Salman Banaei emphasized the need for clearer U.S. regulations to facilitate the adoption of tokenization among small enterprises and community-focused asset-backed securities. According to NS3.AI, such regulatory clarity could significantly enhance the integration of tokenized assets in these sectors. Anne-Sophie Cissey, Chief Administrative Officer at Kaiko, advocated for the establishment of shared standards and improved market structures to support this transition. Recently, the FDIC, Federal Reserve Board, and OCC issued joint guidance on the capital treatment of eligible tokenized securities, highlighting the growing focus on regulatory frameworks in this area.

data.title: Arizona Advances Two Cryptocurrency Bills to House Vote
data.description: Arizona is moving forward with two cryptocurrency-related bills, as they have successfully passed the House Rules Committee and are set for a full House vote. According to ChainCatcher, the first bill, SB1042, proposes allowing state funds to invest up to 10% in cryptocurrencies. The second bill, SB1649, aims to establish a digital asset reserve fund. These legislative developments reflect Arizona's growing interest in integrating digital currencies into its financial framework.

data.title: Global Food Production Concerns Rise Amid U.S. Planting Shortfall
data.description: According to Jin10, Chicago grain prices have surged as U.S. planting areas fall short of expectations, exacerbating concerns over food production amid the ongoing Middle East conflict. The U.S. Department of Agriculture reported that soybean planting areas recorded 84.7 million acres, disappointing traders and leading to further price increases. Wheat planting areas also fell short of analyst expectations at 43.8 million acres. Arlan Suderman, Chief Commodities Economist at StoneX, noted in a report that the total planting area for wheat, corn, and soybeans is approximately one million acres less than anticipated. He added that the reduction in wheat planting aligns with global trends. This disappointing report may heighten risks to global food production, as the month-long U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran has driven up energy and fertilizer prices, threatening global food production. Rising costs not only squeeze farmers' profits but also drive up food inflation, ultimately harming the global economy.

data.title: PeckShield: Crypto hacker losses surged 96% month-over-month to $52 million in March.
data.description: A report released by blockchain security firm PeckShield states that 20 major security incidents occurred in the crypto industry in March 2026, resulting in a total loss of $52 million, a 96% increase compared to $26.5 million in February. However, the report points out that a greater risk lies in "shadow contagion," a chain reaction of systemic risks triggered by attacks. Specifically: 1. Resolv Labs' stablecoin USR experienced a de-pegging incident, with 80 million USR being "unlimitedly issued," causing approximately $25 million in losses and further triggering systemic bad debts in protocols such as Morpho Blue, Euler, and Fluid. 2. Venus suffered a complex attack combining on-chain and off-chain elements, resulting in approximately $2.18 million in bad debt losses. The report also points out that security threats are expanding from on-chain to real-world and social engineering levels. For example, user Sillytuna suffered a combined offline and on-chain attack, resulting in a loss of $24 million; another Kraken big player lost $18 million due to a targeted social engineering attack.

data.title: MGBX will be listed on edgeX (EDGE) spot trading.
data.description: According to official news, MGBX will list edgeX (EDGE) spot trading on April 1, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Deposits will open at 16:00 (SGT) on April 1, 2026. Trading will open at 18:00 (SGT) on April 1, 2026. Withdrawals will open at 19:00 (SGT) on April 3, 2026.
